Title: An update for nginx is now available for openEuler-24.03-LTS-SP3

Notes.Text: An update for nginx is now available for openEuler-24.03-LTS-SP3
Notes.Title: Summary

Notes.Text:
NGINX is a free, open-source, high-performance HTTP server and reverse proxy,  as well as an IMAP/POP3 proxy server.

Security Fix(es):

NGINX Plus and NGINX Open Source have a vulnerability in the ngx_http_proxy_v2_moduleб═and ngx_http_grpc_moduleб═modules. This vulnerability exists when the proxy_http_version to 2б═or grpc_passб═directives are used to proxy HTTP/2 traffic, the ignore_invalid_headersб═directive is set to off, and the large_client_header_buffersб═directive size is larger than 2 megabytes. A remote, unauthenticated attacker, along with conditions beyond their control, could send large headers while creating an upstream request. This may cause a heap-based buffer overflow in the NGINX worker process leading to a restart. Additionally, attackers can execute code on systems with Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR) disabled or when the attacker can bypass ASLR. 


Note: Software versions which have reached End of Technical Support (EoTS) are not evaluated.(CVE-2026-42055)

NGINX Plus and NGINX Open Source have a vulnerability in the ngx_http_charset_moduleб═module. When content is served or proxied through a location block with both source_charsetб═utf-8; and a charsetб═directive (for example, charset koi8-r;) configured, remote, unauthenticated attackers can send requests (in conjunction with conditions beyond their control) to cause a heap buffer over-read in the NGINX worker process, leading to limited disclosure of memory or a restart. 


Note: Software versions which have reached End of Technical Support (EoTS) are not evaluated.(CVE-2026-48142)
Notes.Title: Description

Notes.Text:
An update for nginx is now available for openEuler-24.03-LTS-SP3.

openEuler Security has rated this update as having a security impact of high. A Common Vunlnerability Scoring System(CVSS)base score,which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVElink(s) in the References section.
